question.text: When basis changes from -50 to -40 (contango), the basis is said to be:

answer.text: Strengthening — the spot is gaining relative to futures
Weakening — futures are rising faster than spot
Neutral — no change in relative prices

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Moving from -50 to -40 means basis became less negative → strengthening. Spot is gaining relative to futures (moving towards convergence). **The Trap:** Weakening would be moving from -50 to -60 (more negative). The basis is still negative (contango) but is strengthening. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers strengthening/weakening basis. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Strengthening basis benefits short hedgers (sell futures) because the spot leg gains more value relative to the futures leg — this is a critical concept for hedging performance analysis.
